title: Smoky Chewy Kenyan Maize Bake
description: A smoky, chewy, and deliciously unique Kenyan dish perfect for kids and those with diabetes. It's made with orange blossom water, giving it a distinctive flavor.
instructions: Mix the maize meal, water, vegetable oil, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper in a large bowl until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it's smooth and elastic.
Place the dough on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, and shape it into a rectangular loaf.
Mix the orange blossom water with the butter and brush it on top of the loaf.
Bake the loaf for 25 minutes, then add the chopped onions, carrots, and green beans on top.
Continue baking for another 10 minutes, or until the vegetables are tender.
Remove the loaf from the oven and top it with the shredded cheddar cheese.
Return the loaf to the oven and bake for an additional 2-3 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Let the loaf cool for 5 minutes before slicing and serving.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
